evidence: Primary source: docs/atm-415/atm-417-brand.md (brand direction locked 2026-07-22)
Spec source: contracts/specs/video-storefront.spec.json (locked at ATM-416)
Envelope source: packages/weave-tool/skills/weave-application-lifecycle/ENVELOPE.md (proven stack record)
Negative contract: contracts/negative/public-safe-assets.json
source_refs_present.notes: All research claims cite the locked brand doc, frozen spec, or the envelope's proven-stack record. No unsourced claims.

evidence: FACT: The brand doc is locked and frozen — atm-417-brand.md (status: Brand direction locked 2026-07-22)
FACT: The Next.js+TypeScript+Tailwind stack is proven in the weave-tool envelope (ENVELOPE.md: 4 apps completed)
FACT: commerce-core engine is built and 58/58 tests pass (ATM-416 commit 164785a)
ASSUMPTION: Synthetic cohort funnel probabilities (view 100%, engage 55%, convert 28%, fulfill 95%, refund 4%) are plausible but not derived from real data
ASSUMPTION: Patronage framing ('Back this film') moves conversion vs generic 'Buy' — untested; this is the bounded adaptation candidate
facts_assumptions_split.notes: Facts (locked brand, proven stack, built engine) clearly separated from assumptions (funnel probabilities, CTA framing effect). The assumption list is the honest scope of the synthetic experiment.

evidence: Product uncertainty: LOW — this is a synthetic demo app with locked brand, provided UI, and frozen spec. Product decisions are not uncertain.
User research: patron persona defined in brand doc (not derived from surveys — acknowledged as spec-sourced)
Alternatives considered in spec: all-you-can-watch subscriptions, tip jars, bundle platforms — all rejected in brand doc
Disconfirming evidence: spec.R2 — 'Synthetic patron behavior may differ from real film buyer behavior' — nonclaim explicit
Constraints: synthetic-only scope, public-safe assets, verbatim UI port — all in spec
product_research_coverage_when_uncertain.notes: Product uncertainty is low because the brand direction is locked. The synthetic-only scope means market research is not possible (and not needed for a synthetic demo). The nonclaim covers the gap.

evidence: Product/user coverage: patron persona, frustration story, voice constraints, copy starters — from brand doc
Market positioning: 'The Patron's Channel' vs subscriptions vs tip jars — alternatives enumerated
Value proposition: pay-per-film + maker's name + direct payout qualitative framing
Technical feasibility is a supporting point, not the only coverage
not_technical_feasibility_only.notes: Research covers product positioning, user persona, value proposition, and alternatives — not just technical feasibility.

evidence: Locked brand doc (atm-417-brand.md) — primary source, locked by brand sprint
Frozen spec (video-storefront.spec.json) — derived from brand doc, validated at ATM-416
ENVELOPE.md — proven-stack record from 4 completed lifecycle apps
source_quality.notes: Sources are locked/frozen documents, not ephemeral web searches. Appropriate for a synthetic demo where the brand is pre-defined.

evidence: Target market: indie short-film patrons who want to support specific filmmakers
Alternatives: all-you-can-watch subscriptions (creator gets pennies), tip jars (decoupled from work), bundle platforms (hides who got paid)
Value delivery: pay-per-film + creator name + qualitative payout framing
Positioning: 'The Patron's Channel' — distinct from all alternatives enumerated
product_market_coverage.notes: Product-market coverage is complete for a synthetic synthetic demo. All alternatives considered; positioning is sharp.

evidence: R1: UI port deviation — mitigated by impeccable gate >=7/10, zero P0
R2: synthetic patron behavior may differ from real — nonclaim in every verdict
R3: single-item cart removes cross-sell signal — documented simplification
Public-safe scan contract: fictional names, no real footage
risk_identification.notes: All risks in the spec with mitigations. No surprises from the research phase.

evidence: Stack proven: Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind in ENVELOPE.md (4 apps completed)
Engine built: commerce-core 58/58 tests pass (ATM-416)
UI provided: docs/atm-415/ui/onereel — port not redesign
No unknown technical risks in the envelope
technical_feasibility.notes: Fully feasible. Stack is proven, engine is built, UI is provided. No open technical questions.

evidence: FACT claims: stack proven, engine built, brand locked, UI provided
ASSUMPTION claims: funnel probabilities, CTA effect on conversion
NONCLAIM: synthetic cohort does not predict real patron behavior
claim_taxonomy.notes: Claims are labeled and separated. Facts cite specific artifacts; assumptions are acknowledged as untested.

evidence: All decisions made: stack selected, UI sourced, catalog defined, KPI frozen
Next stage (selection) has no open questions to answer — everything is in the spec
Blocking question if any: none — brand is locked, UI is provided, engine is built
decision_readiness.notes: Ready for selection. No open research questions remain.

overall_notes: Research is fully grounded in the locked brand doc and frozen spec. The synthetic scope limits research to the pre-defined brand rather than live market data — this is appropriate and the nonclaim is explicit. All hard gates pass. Decision-ready.
